acceptance_criteria: attacks_reproducibility_qc.tsv contains required per-row fields and header
qc_decision.md contains explicit cross-references and a worked calculation
Regenerated artifacts preserved original numeric results (minimum_baseline_margin unchanged within tolerance)
dependencies: No external data; only the embedded records in the script and the TSV file.
extra_model_fields.notes: Planned changes are minimal and targeted at improving auditability. The risk of changing numeric results is low if tests verify parity; if a numeric difference is detected, document it in qc_decision.md and explain the cause.

acceptance_criteria: attacks_reproducibility_qc.tsv contains required per-row fields and header

extra_model_fields.notes: Planned changes are minimal and targeted at improving auditability. The risk of changing numeric results is low if tests verify parity; if a numeric difference is detected, document it in qc_decision.md and explain the cause.

planned_changes: Inspect atac_reproducibility_qc.tsv and confirm it contains the required per-row fields (see actionable_feedback). If missing, modify reproduce_atac_qc.py to output the missing columns and re-generate the TSV.

Implement a --verify or --test flag in reproduce_atac_qc.py that (a) computes per-metric normalized contributions and observed_risk_score for each embedded record, (b) asserts equality (within numerical tolerance) with values written to the TSV, and (c) asserts that the minimum baseline margin matches the recorded 10.771188978736149 to within tolerance.

Add a compact header/metadata block to atac_reproducibility_qc.tsv describing the formula (min-max normalization, mean aggregation), THRESHOLD, WARN_BAND, BUDGET, and explicit numeric application of O1/O2/O3 (including algebraic expression for O3 as margin multiplier), plus a reference to the verification command.

Update qc_decision.md to include line/record references to TSV rows, a worked numeric example for one record showing each normalization step, and an explicit statement of how O3 was interpreted (and the numeric effect).

Run the updated script locally to regenerate artifacts and execute the verification flag; commit updated artifacts and include a short changelog entry in qc_decision.md noting the added tests and TSV header.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address verifier limitation by confirming/adding TSV row-level citations, adding reproducibility checks and tests to the script, clarifying O3 interpretation, and adding compact documentation to the TSV and memo to make verification end-to-end and one-command reproducible.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Address verifier limitation by confirming/adding TSV row-level citations, adding reproducibility checks and tests to the script, clarifying O3 interpretation, and adding compact documentation to the TSV and memo to make verification end-to-end and one-command reproducible.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The baseline attempt meets the artifact contract and documents a reproducible packet-only scoring approach. The grader and verifier agree the calculations (margins, classifications, and remediation cost checks) are consistent with the script and memo. Remaining limitation: the TSV artifact was only available as binary/metadata in previews, so the presence of explicit row-level citations and per-row intermediate fields (per-metric normalized contributions, observed_risk_score, margin, and source citation) could not be independently confirmed. Recommend small local fixes to increase ease of verification and end-to-end reproducibility.
